title: 10 best herbal decoctions for altitude sickness
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for altitude sickness are natural remedies made by steeping herbs in hot water, which can provide relief from the symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS). These decoctions work by improving oxygen delivery to the body's cells and reducing inflammation. Examples of helpful herbal decoctions include ginkgo biloba, which improves blood flow and oxygenation, and Rhodiola rosea, which helps reduce stress and fatigue. By using these natural remedies, travelers can enjoy their mountain adventures without worrying about AMS, and local communities can continue to thrive in high-altitude regions with minimal risk of illness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: King of bitters decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it contains a unique blend of herbs that work in harmony to alleviate symptoms. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and congestion caused by high elevation, while its expectorant qualities relieve coughing and shortness of breath. Additionally, the herbs' antioxidant effects help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage triggered by altitude changes, promoting a smoother adjustment to new elevations. By supporting the body's natural response to altitude changes, King of bitters decoctions helps alleviate symptoms and promote acclimatization.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: King of Bitters decoction's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by high altitude conditions, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue.

Cardiovascular tonic effects: The cardiovascular tonic effects of King of Bitters decoction help to improve blood circulation and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, which can help to mitigate the effects of hypoxia (oxygen deficiency) at high altitudes.

Digestive and anti-inflammatory properties: King of Bitters decoction's digestive and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and protect the gut lining, reducing symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea that are common in altitude sickness, while also alleviating inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of King of Bitters contain bioactive compounds like gentiopicrin, which help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and improving oxygenation.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are underground stems that store nutrients, and they are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in managing symptoms of altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried andrographis paniculata root powder per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured root powder with 250ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15-20 minutes before straining and discarding the solids.
Filter the decoction through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any remaining residue.
Store the prepared decoction in a clean glass bottle and refrigerate for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it contains compounds that help to dilate blood vessels, increasing oxygen flow to the body's tissues. The decoction also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the brain and other organs, which can occur as a result of high altitudes. Additionally, maidenhair tree decoctions have been shown to stimulate the immune system, helping the body to adapt to the lower oxygen levels found at high elevations. By promoting healthy circulation and reducing inflammation, maidenhair tree decoctions can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, making it an effective natural remedy for those who venture to high altitudes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba decoctions help protect against oxidative stress caused by high-altitude conditions, which can lead to inflammation and tissue damage.

Circulatory stimulant properties: The decoction's ability to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow helps to increase oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, reducing symptoms of altitude sickness such as headache, fatigue, and dizziness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling in the lungs and other organs that can occur at high altitudes, which can lead to respiratory distress and other complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
Barks: Containing bioactive flavonoids and terpenes, the barks have been traditionally used to treat altitude-related illnesses due to their ability to improve blood circulation and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Rinse 10-15g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with distilled water to remove impurities.
Combine the rinsed ginkgo biloba leaves with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 20-30 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids.
Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator and consume within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Golden root decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of high-altitude maladies. The decoction's adaptogenic properties help the body acclimate to changes in oxygen levels, reducing the severity of headaches, fatigue, and nausea associated with altitude sickness. Additionally, golden root's antioxidant activity helps combat oxidative stress caused by high-altitude environments, further enhancing its therapeutic effects.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Golden Root's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to high-altitude conditions by regulating cortisol levels, reducing stress, and improving oxygen delivery to the tissues.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of Golden Root decoctions help mitigate oxidative stress caused by high altitude exposure, which can damage cells and tissues, by neutralizing free radicals and protecting against cell damage.

Vasodilatory effects: Golden Root's vasodilatory properties help improve blood flow to the body's tissues, including those in the lungs and brain, which is essential for efficient oxygenation and adaptation to high-altitude conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the golden root plant contain a high concentration of glycosides, which have been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve oxygenation in the body.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the golden root plant are rich in sesquiterpenoids, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried rhodiola rosea root powder into a small saucepan.
Add one cup of boiling water to the saucepan and stir to combine ingredients.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes allowing herbs to infuse into liquid.
Strain decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a large cup or mug.
Allow mixture to cool slightly before drinking while warm within an hour after ascent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common symptom of high-altitude conditions. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, can help to soothe and calm the body's tissues, reducing swelling and discomfort. Additionally, licorice decoctions may also help to alleviate symptoms of acute mountain sickness, including headaches and fatigue, by promoting healthy digestion and supporting respiratory function.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has been shown to help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common underlying factors in altitude sickness, by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals.
Isomaltose: This sugar molecule helps to thin out mucus and facilitate its clearance from the lungs, alleviating congestion and shortness of breath that can occur at high altitudes.

Licoricidin: This compound has been found to have a vasodilatory effect, helping to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, lightheadedness, and fatigue often experienced during altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots have been traditionally used to treat altitude sickness due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help alleviate respiratory distress.
Barks: The bark of the licorice plant contains flavonoids and polyphenols that are believed to aid in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with high-altitude conditions.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 20g of dried licorice root and add it to a large pot.
Combine the licorice root with 2 liters of water in the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes, then remove from heat.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds that work synergistically to combat the symptoms of high-altitude exposure. The diuretic properties of dandelion help to reduce fluid buildup in the lungs, alleviating respiratory distress, while its anti-inflammatory effects soothe irritated tissues and mucous membranes. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant properties protect against oxidative stress, which can exacerbate altitude sickness.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion decoction can help alleviate fluid buildup in the lungs caused by high altitudes, reducing congestion and discomfort.

Cardiovascular tonic property: The herbal decoction's ability to strengthen the heart and blood vessels helps maintain healthy blood pressure and flow, which is crucial for coping with altitude-induced hypoxia.
Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling in the lungs and airways caused by high altitudes, making breathing easier and more comfortable.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant contain a unique blend of minerals and compounds that help to improve oxygenation in the body, making it an effective remedy for alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the dandelion plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the lungs and other affected areas, making them a key component in treating altitude sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh or dried dandelion roots and wash them thoroughly in cold running water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and place them in a large pot filled with 500ml of distilled water.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator at a temperature of 2-8°c for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense decoctions helps with altitude sickness because they contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress. At high altitudes, the lower air pressure can cause inflammation in the lungs and brain, leading to symptoms of altitude sickness. The anti-inflammatory properties of frankincense may help to alleviate these symptoms by reducing the body's response to this inflammation, allowing the body to adapt more easily to the high altitude environment.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Frankincense contains boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, helping to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headache, fatigue, and nausea.

Oxygenation promotion: Frankincense has been found to increase oxygenation of the body's tissues by improving blood flow and reducing blood viscosity, which can help counteract the decreased oxygen levels at high altitudes that contribute to altitude sickness.

Adaptogenic effects: Frankincense is considered an adaptogen, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and environmental changes, including those caused by high altitude. It may help regulate cortisol levels and reduce anxiety and fatigue associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, contain bioactive compounds like sesquiterpenes and diterpenes that help alleviate symptoms associated with high altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function.

Barks: The barks of the frankincense plant are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which can help to improve blood flow to the lungs and reduce oxidative stress caused by high altitude conditions, making them effective for treating altitude-related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one tablespoon of dried boswellia serrata powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for ten minutes and then strain it through a coffee filter.
Add one teaspoon of honey to sweeten the decoction and stir well.
Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.
Drink two cups of the decoction per day, morning and evening, for at least five consecutive days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it increases blood flow to the brain and reduces inflammation. The ginsenosides in ginseng have been shown to improve oxygenation of tissues, which is particularly important at high altitudes where the air pressure is lower. Additionally, ginseng has antioxidant properties that help protect against damage caused by free radicals, which can accumulate at high elevations. By improving circulation and reducing oxidative stress, ginseng decoctions may help alleviate symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and shortness of breath associated with altitude sickness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginseng decoctions' antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress caused by high altitudes, which can contribute to altitude sickness.

Cerebroprotective and cardioprotective effects: The decoction's ability to protect the brain and heart from damage helps to alleviate symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue associated with altitude sickness.

Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng decoctions' adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to high-altitude conditions by stimulating circulation, improving oxygen delivery to tissues, and enhancing the body's natural response to stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of ginsenosides, compounds responsible for ginseng's adaptogenic properties, which help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, contain bioactive compounds that can help regulate blood pressure and improve oxygenation in high-altitude environments.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried panax ginseng root into a clean glass container for every cup of water.
Heat filtered water to boiling point and let it cool down to 80-90°c for at least 30 minutes.
Add the measured ginseng root to the cooled water and steep for 20-30 minutes in a covered container.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Let the decoction cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it and consuming within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it is a natural adaptogen that aids in stress reduction, increasing tolerance to high altitudes. The decoction's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by the body's adjustment to lower oxygen levels. Additionally, ashwagandha's ability to improve cardiovascular function and increase red blood cell count helps to counteract the effects of altitude sickness, allowing the body to better adapt to changes in oxygen levels.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Ashwagandha helps the body adapt to stress caused by high altitudes, reducing symptoms such as fatigue, weakness, and anxiety.

Oxygenation and circulation enhancement: Ashwagandha's ability to improve oxygen delivery to tissues and enhance blood circulation can help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as shortness of breath and dizziness.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help protect cells from damage caused by high-altitude hypoxia (low oxygen levels), while its anti-inflammatory effects may reduce swelling and discomfort in the lungs and other affected areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as fatigue and shortness of breath.
Barks: Contain astringent properties that aid in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with high-altitude exposure.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ashwagandha root powder into a small heat-resistant cup.
Boil two cups of water and pour it over the herb mixture in the cup.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 15-20 minutes allowing the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before consuming one quarter cup three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange decoctions helps with altitude sickness because it contains bioactive compounds that help to increase oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. The decoction's citric acid content also enhances the body's natural production of nitric oxide, which widens blood vessels and improves circulation. This increased blood flow helps to reduce symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches, fatigue, and shortness of breath, allowing the body to better adapt to high elevations.
remedy_properties: Vasoconstriction-promoting compounds: Bitter orange contains vasopressin, which helps to constrict blood vessels and increase blood pressure, reducing the likelihood of cerebral edema and other complications associated with high altitudes.

Adaptogenic flavonoids: The flavonoids present in bitter orange, such as hesperidin and naringenin, help the body adapt to stress caused by altitude changes, reducing oxidative damage and inflammation, and improving cardiovascular function.

Anti-inflammatory sesquiterpenes: Bitter orange contains sesquiterpenes like limonene, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling in the brain and other tissues, common symptoms of acute mountain sickness (AMS).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and glycosides, which help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness by reducing inflammation and improving circulation.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and saponins, the bark helps to dilate blood vessels, improving oxygen delivery to the body's tissues at high altitudes.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried citrus x aurantium peel in a quantity of 2-3 tablespoons and store it in an airtight container.
Measure out 250ml of distilled water into a pot and bring it to a boil for 10 minutes.
Add the dried citrus peel to the boiling water, then reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding solids and retaining 200ml of liquid.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature and refrigerate it for at least 2 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon decoctions helps with altitude sickness because its active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde and epicatechin, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of high-altitude exposure. The decoction's warming and soothing effects can also aid in reducing fatigue, joint pain, and headaches commonly associated with altitude sickness. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's ability to improve blood circulation may help to counteract the decreased oxygen levels at higher elevations, making it a valuable natural remedy for those who frequently travel to high-altitude regions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ceylon cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the body, which can exacerbate altitude sickness symptoms such as headaches and nausea.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant activity of Ceylon cinnamon may help neutralize free radicals generated by high-altitude conditions, reducing oxidative stress and damage to tissues.

Vasodilatory properties: Ceylon cinnamon's ability to relax blood vessels may help increase blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, which can alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as fatigue and shortness of breath.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing symptoms of altitude sickness.
Rhyzomes: Contain volatile oils that help reduce inflammation and improve oxygenation of tissues, alleviating altitude-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a clean glass container.
Boil one liter of water in a pot and pour it over the herb mixture.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors and active compounds to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean container.
Allow the liquid to cool before consuming one cup of the warm decoction three times daily.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat altitude sickness with herbal decoctions is to combine Ginkgo biloba and Eleuthero (Siberian Ginseng) to improve oxygen delivery and enhance respiratory function. Drink a warm decoction of Rhodiola rosea, which helps to reduce stress and fatigue caused by high elevations. Additionally, consume a tea made from Gentiana scabra, known for its ability to ease nausea and headaches often associated with altitude sickness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to altitude sickness that are treated with herbal decoctions are conditions such as anemia, bronchitis, and chronic fatigue syndrome. Herbs like Ginkgo biloba, Eleuthero, and Ashwagandha help increase oxygenation of the body, improve circulation, and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms similar to those experienced at high elevations. These herbal remedies have been traditionally used in traditional Chinese medicine and Ayurvedic practices to treat various conditions that affect energy levels and overall well-being.
